description: Tier-2 specialist — ingests lcov/cobertura + git blame age + call-graph, ranks untested code by impact×exposure, delivers top-N not noise.

cases.prompt: I have an lcov coverage report and git blame output for a legacy Node.js codebase. The payment processing module (src/payments/) is uncovered, was last touched 3 years ago, and is called by 12 other modules. The logging utility (src/logger/) is also uncovered. Which should I test first and why?
cases.notes: Payments > logger because of higher fan-in + revenue impact. Must explain impact × exposure reasoning.

cases.prompt: Audit our `src/` directory for untested error paths — specifically try/catch blocks whose catch clause is never exercised by any test.
cases.notes: Must produce a list of uncovered catch blocks with file:line locations.

cases.prompt: The coverage report shows 847 uncovered lines. Can you report all 847 as findings?
cases.notes: Must refuse a 847-item dump. Must propose prioritized top-N ranked by impact × exposure.
